value.body:
Found during a direct Node API audit of runtime source (`packages/**/src`, excluding tests, scripts, vendor, and generated protocol files).

Problem:
`@cycle/codex-app-server` spawns and manages the Codex app-server process through direct Node APIs instead of the Effect child-process services used elsewhere in the repo.

Evidence:
- `packages/codex-app-server/src/client.ts:1` imports `node:child_process`.
- `packages/codex-app-server/src/client.ts:186-190` converts string chunks with `Buffer.from`.
- `packages/codex-app-server/src/client.ts:210-226` merges env from `process.env`, calls `spawn(...)`, and branches on `process.platform`.

Suggested remediation:
- Rework the child process transport to use `effect/unstable/process` `ChildProcessSpawner` or a narrow app-server process adapter.
- Thread env/platform in from callers rather than reading `process.env`/`process.platform`.
- Replace `Buffer.from` with a portable text encoder path or typed-array conversion helper.

Acceptance criteria:
- Codex app-server client runtime source no longer imports `node:child_process` or reads `process.env`/`process.platform` directly.
- Spawn errors and stderr line handling keep the current public error behavior.
- Desktop/API callers provide the required Effect process layer or adapter.

frontmatter.title: Replace Codex app-server child_process client with Effect process/platform services

value.title: Replace Codex app-server child_process client with Effect process/platform services
